items.title: Regression target modeling
items.description: Minimizing MSE during training drives predictions toward E[Y | X] under standard assumptions; understanding expectation clarifies what a point forecast represents.

items.title: Feature normalization
items.description: Computing column means and variances to standardize inputs uses sample estimates of E[X] and Var(X), stabilizing optimization and making regularization comparable across features.

items.title: Bias–variance analysis
items.description: Decomposing generalization error into bias² and variance of an estimator relies directly on E[·] and Var(·) of predictions across training samples.

items.title: Monte Carlo and stochastic gradients
items.description: Mini-batch loss averages approximate full-dataset expectations; linearity of expectation justifies splitting complex objectives into per-sample terms that SGD optimizes.
